Empires & Puzzles Sir Roostley Review: Is The Golden Chicken Still Golden?

Is Sir Roostley still worth your darts in 2026? We dive deep into the stats and skills of the original Springvale paladin to see if his chain attack still clucks or if he's just a nugget in a world of behemoths.
Sir Roostley Review: The Golden Chicken That Crossed The Road To Obscurity
Sir Roostley: The Golden Chicken That Crossed The Road To Obscurity
Sir Roostley is a 5-star Legendary Holy hero from the
family, originally released way back in 2020. He represents a simpler time in

, where a "Chain Attack" was a mind-blowing mechanic and self-debuffs were the "balance" for supposed power. As a Paladin, he was once meant to be a sturdy tank or flank, soaking up hits while zapping enemies with his laser lance.

original Springvale hero without a costume (poor chicken), looking a bit like a rotisserie bird in a field of dragons and gods. He brings an Average speed chain attack that can theoretically hit everyone, provided the stars align, the wind is blowing east, and your opponent's tank is still alive.

Sir Roostley is quite literally a giant rooster in shining armor. It's ridiculous, it's colorful, and it captures the whimsical essence of the Springvale event perfectly. He wields a lance that looks like a giant green carrot (or maybe just a festive lance, but let's go with carrot). His expression is one of supreme confidence—the kind of unearned confidence you have before you step into a war battle and get one-shot by a modern sniper. It’s charming, classic E&P art that doesn't take itself too seriously.

This was decent in 2020. In 2026, where modern support heroes often have 1300+ attack, this is barely a scratch.
These durability stats are dangerously low. Even with his Paladin class defensive boost, he is extremely fragile. A modern sniper can likely delete him without even needing a defense down ailment.
Compared to modern behemoths boasting 1300+ stats across the board, Sir Roostley is fighting with one wing tied behind his back.
is a "Chain Attack" mechanic. It hits the target for 310% damage and then chains to adjacent enemies.
It works like electricity. If you hit the Tank (position 3), it chains to Flanks (2 and 4), and then to Wings (1 and 5), hitting everyone.
, if you hit a Wing (1), it chains to the Flank (2), then the Tank (3), and so on.

The chain breaks if it hits a "gap" (a dead hero) or if the attack misses (due to blind or dodge). If the enemy Tank is dead—which is often the case by the time an Average speed hero fires—hitting a Flank will
hit that Flank and the Wing next to it. You lose half your damage potential immediately.
. This is uncleansable. Why? Because in 2020, hitting 5 enemies for 310% was considered "too strong." Today, heroes hit everyone for 500% and
buffs. This drawback is now just an insult to injury.
Average (10 tiles). With a Level 23 Mana Troop, this drops to 9 tiles.
Often, the tank is dead. Hitting a flank hits 2-3 enemies max. 310% x 2 =
. For an Average speed hero, that is abysmal.
The -20% attack reduction means his subsequent slash attacks or a second special firing (if he lives that long) will be significantly weaker.

Upon release, Sir Roostley was "A-Tier." Players loved the chain mechanic and the high damage ceiling. He was a viable tank because he punished bad boards; if you couldn't kill him, he'd fire and hit your whole team.

Power creep has not been kind to the Rooster. His damage percentages are low compared to modern "Hit All" heroes who essentially do what he does but better, faster, and without condition. The "Chain" mechanic is now a liability rather than a feature, as it's too easily disrupted by the flow of battle (dead enemies).
He is effectively a bench warmer for most rosters. He might see play in
wars if you literally have no other Yellow options, or in
, but he is too slow and too weak for serious PvP.
He is incredibly squishy. He needs every point of defense to survive a single hit from a modern sniper. The Paladin class talent (Protect) synergizes with high defense.
His only value is damage. Making him tanky means he hits like a wet noodle, especially with his self-attack down.
Maximizes the damage of his special skill. If he's going to fire, you want it to hurt.
He will die. Quickly. A stiff breeze or a minion hit might take him out.
If you insist on using him, go
. His only role is to deal damage. If he's not doing damage, he's doing nothing. You just have to pray he fires before he explodes.
In a perfect scenario (Rush war, enemy team full health), he can hit all 5 enemies for decent damage.
The chance to get +25% defense when hit is one of the better defensive talents.
He looks like a knighted chicken. That's a strength in my book.
The chain breaks if enemies are dead or if he misses.
-20% attack reduction is a painful penalty for a skill that isn't even overpowered anymore.
He is statistically irrelevant compared to 2025/2026 heroes.
Too slow for the impact he provides.
This is his only real home. At Very Fast speed, he might fire before the enemy tank dies, ensuring a hit on all 5 enemies.
He's okay for clearing map stages where enemies don't hit back hard.

Sir Roostley + Killhare + Master Lepus. The family bonus gives them all +20% attack, which helps mitigate their various self-defense/attack debuffs. It's a glass cannon team that aims to wipe the enemy in one turn.
Sir Roostley + Vivica (C2) + Ogima. In Rush wars, pair him with a defense down hero (like Vivica) to maximize his chain damage.

He is a collector's item. Do not Limit Break him. Do not give him Alpha Aethers. He serves as a reminder of how far the game has come. If you have duplicates, he is excellent fodder for the
The community has had a love-hate relationship with the Rooster over the years.
"I think he’s a mixed bag... The chain idea is horrible as is, it would make much more sense if the damage is increased after every chain."
- A common sentiment regarding his mechanic.
"He was never a good hero. He never did enough damage for his speed and his chain attack is very very deceptible to blindness."
"Is Sir Roostley worth Summoning For? Not really."
- The poll results speak for themselves.

Sir Roostley is a relic. He's a fun concept—a noble chicken knight—trapped in a stat block from half a decade ago. His special skill, while visually cool, is mechanically flawed for offensive raids where you

to kill the tank first. With the release of massive powerhouses in the Springvale family (like Archie) and no costume to save him, Roostley is unfortunately more "chicken dinner" than "winner winner."
Bench him, or trade him. But maybe keep one copy just to hear him cluck.
A comprehensive review of Sir Roostley, the 5-star Holy hero from the Springvale family. We analyze his stats, special skill, and viability in the modern Empires & Puzzles meta.
